message:
fix(scripts): sum all test-result lines instead of matching only the first

Every scripts/check_*.py validator parsed Rust test output with
re.search(r"test result: ok\. (\d+) passed", ...) which only finds
the first "test result:" line. When cargo runs multiple test harnesses
(unit, integration, doc-tests), only the first harness count was
captured, under-reporting the actual pass count.

Fix: replace re.search → re.findall + sum(int(m) for m in matches)
across all ~30 check_*.py scripts, so total test counts reflect every
harness that ran.

Also adds new test suites:
- tests/test_check_degraded_mode_audit.py
- tests/test_check_provenance_gate.py
- tests/test_check_schema_migration.py
And expands tests/test_check_network_guard.py

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

+def parse_rust_test_summary(output):
+    running = sum(int(match) for match in re.findall(r"running (\d+) test(?:s)?", output))
+    result_matches = TEST_RESULT_RE.findall(output)
+    passed = sum(int(match[0]) for match in result_matches)
+    failed = sum(int(match[1]) for match in result_matches)
+    filtered = sum(int(match[4]) for match in result_matches)
+    return {
+        "running": running,
+        "passed": passed,
+        "failed": failed,
+        "filtered": filtered,
+    }
+
+
+def summarize_failure_output(output, max_lines=6):
+    cleaned_lines = [
+        ANSI_ESCAPE_RE.sub("", line).strip() for line in output.splitlines()
+    ]
+    cleaned_lines = [line for line in cleaned_lines if line]
+    error_start = next(
+        (idx for idx, line in enumerate(cleaned_lines) if "error" in line.lower()),
+        None,
+    )
+    snippet = (
+        cleaned_lines[error_start:error_start + max_lines]
+        if error_start is not None
+        else cleaned_lines[:max_lines]
+    )
+    return " | ".join(snippet)
+
+
+def select_failure_excerpt(*outputs):
+    for output in outputs:
+        excerpt = summarize_failure_output(output)
+        if excerpt:
+            return excerpt
+    return ""
+
